rationale_for_baseline_tier: Post-viral fatigue is not itself an emergency, but it is a diagnosis of exclusion: the patient needs baseline bloods and (given new post-viral exertional intolerance) an ECG, plus a red-flag screen, so prompt (1–2 week, sooner if concerned) in-person review/work-up is appropriate (T2). Myocarditis/cardiac features carry a hard 000/ED (T5) safety-net; organic red flags route to urgent GP.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain, breathlessness at rest or rapidly worsening on exertion, a racing or irregular heartbeat, fainting on exertion, or new ankle swelling
escalation_edges.rationale: Possible post-viral myocarditis — cannot be excluded remotely; potentially life-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get chest pain, breathlessness at rest, a racing or irregular heartbeat, faint on exertion, or new ankle swelling — call 000 or go to ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unexplained weight loss, drenching night sweats, persistent measured fever, new lumps, or new weakness/numbness

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with severe functional impairment
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Consider practical support (meals, energy conservation) and a clear pacing plan; low threshold for review.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like post-viral fatigue. Let's arrange some blood tests and a heart tracing to be sure nothing else is going on, and in the meantime use strict pacing — rest before you get exhausted and avoid pushing through.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP review to arrange bloods ± ECG within 1–2 weeks; 000 for cardiac features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000/ED for chest pain, breathlessness at rest, palpitations or fainting at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Post-viral fatigue (diagnosis of exclusion); cardiac and organic red flags screened negative; baseline bloods + ECG requested; pacing advised; cardiac safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Labelling post-viral fatigue with no work-up or cardiac safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Post-viral myocarditis is mislabelled as fatigue and the patient deteriorates or has a fatal arrhythmia.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No baseline bloods / organic red-flag safety-net, missing a treatable cause.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for stable post-viral fatigue with a negative cardiac screen — usually unnecessary (though a low threshold given the myocarditis question is reasonable).
